differentials.name: following radiotherapy of giant cell tumor / lymphoma / breast cancer
differentials.raw: following radiotherapy of giant cell tumor / lymphoma / breast cancer

differentials.name: underlying benign lesion: Paget disease
differentials.raw: underlying benign lesion: Paget disease (common); giant cell tumor, bone infarct, osteomyelitis, desmoplastic fibroma, enchondroma, fibrous dysplasia (rare)

differentials.name: dedifferentiation of low-grade chondrosarcoma Histo:   spectrum of well to poorly differentiated fibrous tissue proliferation; will not produce osteoid / chondroid / osseous matrix
differentials.raw: dedifferentiation of low-grade chondrosarcoma Histo:   spectrum of well to poorly differentiated fibrous tissue proliferation; will not produce osteoid / chondroid / osseous matrix

differentials.name: localized painful mass Location:   tubular bones in young, flat bones in older patients; femur (40%), tibia (16%) (about knee in 30-50%), jaw, pelvis (9%); rare in small bones of hand + feet or spinal column Site:   eccentric at diaphyseal-metaphyseal junction into metaphysis; intramedullary / periosteal
differentials.raw: localized painful mass Location:   tubular bones in young, flat bones in older patients; femur (40%), tibia (16%) (about knee in 30-50%), jaw, pelvis (9%); rare in small bones of hand + feet or spinal column Site:   eccentric at diaphyseal-metaphyseal junction into metaphysis; intramedullary / periosteal

differentials.name: PERIOSTEAL FIBROSARCOMA =  rare tumor arising from periosteal connective tissue
differentials.raw: PERIOSTEAL FIBROSARCOMA =  rare tumor arising from periosteal connective tissue

differentials.name: Aneurysmal bone cyst
differentials.raw: Aneurysmal bone cyst (eccentric blown-out appearance with rapid progression)

differentials.name: Malignant giant cell tumor
differentials.raw: Malignant giant cell tumor (begins in metaphysis extending toward joint)

context: Age:   predominantly in 3rd-5th decade (range, 8-88 years); M÷F = 1÷1 Metastases to:   lung, lymph nodes √  well-defined lucent bone lesion √  thin expanded cortex √  aggressive osteolysis with geographic / ragged / permeative bone destruction + wide zone of transition √  occasionally large osteolytic lesion with cortical destruction, periosteal reaction + soft- tissue invasion √  sequestration of bone may be present (DDx: eosinophilic granuloma, bacterial granuloma) √  sparse periosteal proliferation (uncommon) √  intramedullary discontinuous spread √  no calcification DDx:   malignant fibrou
